gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP window collapse via low observation cardinality enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX falls back to oldestObservation when pool cardinality=1, collapsing the 900s TWAP window to ~12s (1 block), allowing a manipulated tick to set amountOutMinimum near zero
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild, extracting up to ~91% of the TitanX-denominated Torus value from the protocol's build interval allocation

live_preconditions.name: titanXIntervalsBuild[53061].amountAllocated > 0
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — parameterized_views only sampled indices 0 and 1, not 53061

live_preconditions.name: TORUS/TITANX pool observationCardinality == 1
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — pool address not directly in live_context; must be read from torusToken.titanXTorusPool() then pool.slot0()
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: POOL=$(cast call 0xb47f575807fc5466285e1277ef8acfbb5c6686e8 'titanXTorusPool()(address)' --rpc-url mainnet); cast call $POOL 'slot0()((uint160,int24,uint16,uint16,uint16,uint8,bool))' --rpc-url mainnet

live_preconditions.name: TORUS/TITANX pool last observation age < 900s (attacker can be last trader)
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — depends on pool activity and cardinality
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: POOL=$(cast call 0xb47f575807fc5466285e1277ef8acfbb5c6686e8 'titanXTorusPool()(address)' --rpc-url mainnet); cast call $POOL 'observations(uint256)((uint32,int56,uint160,bool))' 0 --rpc-url mainnet

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: secondsAgo is capped to oldestObservation; if cardinality=1 and last swap was 1 block ago, secondsAgo collapses to ~12, making the TWAP equivalent to spot price at the manipulated tick
amountOutMinimum is derived solely from getTorusQuoteForTitanX; if that returns near-zero due to TWAP collapse, the swap executes with no effective slippage protection
Public entry point with no caller restriction beyond nonReentrant and buildTitanXIntervalUpdate; any unprivileged address can trigger the vulnerable swap path

With cardinality=1, (observationIndex+1)%1 == 0, so it always reads observations[0]; secondsAgo = block.timestamp - observations[0].timestamp, which equals time since last swap — attacker-controllable

attacker_path.actor: unprivileged EOA with sufficient TORUS or TITANX to move the pool tick
attacker_inputs: Block N: large swap in TORUS/TITANX pool (TORUS→TITANX direction) to push tick to very low TORUS price, writing a new observation at timestamp T_N
Block N+1: call swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(deadline) — or front-run a legitimate caller's pending tx
call_sequence: Block N: attacker → IUniswapV3Pool(titanXTorusPool).swap(...) — manipulates tick, writes observation[0].timestamp = T_N
→ OracleLibrary.consult(pool, 12): returns arithmeticMeanTick reflecting manipulated price
→ ISwapRouter.exactInput with amountOutMinimum ≈ 0 executes at any price
Block N+1 (same bundle): attacker back-runs the swap to restore price, capturing the price impact
state_before: pool last observation timestamp = T_N (attacker's manipulation block)
state_after: Protocol receives far fewer TORUS than fair market value for the TitanX spent
Attacker captures the price impact delta as MEV profit
currInterval.amountBurned set to amountAllocated — interval cannot be replayed

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX from protocol's build interval allocation (totalTitanXForBuild)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Price impact profit from sandwich: up to ~91% of fair TORUS value for the swapped TitanX half (titanXToUse/2)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol's build interval TitanX is spent at a manipulated rate; liquidity added in _addLiquidityBuild is also impaired by the low torusPurchased amount
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is not entitled to any portion of the protocol's build allocation; the slippage check (titanXToTorusSlippage=10) is applied to a manipulated quote, not fair market price, making it ineffective

setup: Assert IUniswapV3Pool(pool).slot0() observationCardinality == 1; if > 1, reject finding
Assert titanXIntervalsBuild[53061].amountAllocated > 0 and amountBurned == 0; if not, advance interval via distributeTitanXForBuilding
transaction_sequence: vm.roll(block.number); deal TORUS to attacker; attacker swaps large TORUS→TITANX in pool to push tick down
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If pool observationCardinality > 1 and getOldestObservationSecondsAgo consistently returns >= 900, the TWAP window does not collapse and the finding must be rejected. Also reject if titanXIntervalsBuild[53061].amountAllocated == 0 and no new interval can be funded without privileged action.

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Two hard gates are set false: live_state_supports_preconditions (pool cardinality not confirmed from live_context) and not_external_dependency_only (the attack requires the Uniswap V3 pool to have cardinality=1, which is a pool-level state not controlled by this contract). These unknowns require a fork test to resolve before promotion.
